sidebar.noReposBody: Add a single repo or scan a folder to get started.
sidebar.confirmRemove: Remove "{{name}}" from codesight? Files on disk are not affected.
sidebar.deleteConfirm: Remove "{{name}}" from codesight? Files on disk are not affected.
sidebar.emptyGroupHint: Drop a repo here to assign this tag
sidebar.dropNoRepoBody: The dropped folder(s) don't contain a .git directory.
sidebar.dragHint: Drag to resize · double-click to reset

tagManager.title: Manage tags
tagManager.subtitle: Group repositories with colored labels
tagManager.confirmDelete: Delete tag "{{name}}"? Repository files are not affected.
tagManager.noTags: No tags yet. Create one to start grouping.

timeline.title: Commit timeline
timeline.velocityHint: Smoothed delivery pace — 4-{{period}} moving average. Cuts through spikes to show the underlying trend.

tags.title: Releases / Tags
tags.subtitle: Annotated and lightweight tags
tags.noTags: This repository has no tags yet.

contributors.title: Contributors
contributors.subtitle: Everyone who has committed
contributors.cohortHint: Active / new / returning authors per month

search.title: Commit search
search.subtitle: Filter commits by message, author, date or path

ownership.title: Code ownership
ownership.subtitle: Bus factor & primary authorship
ownership.busFactorHint: Min authors covering 50% of code changes

alerts.title: Heads up
alerts.busFactorOne: {{name}} alone carries 50%+ of code changes — single point of failure.
alerts.highConcentration: {{count}} files have a single owner with {{pct}}%+ share — bus risk.
alerts.alumni: {{count}} contributors have not committed in {{days}}+ days — knowledge may be drifting.

graph.title: Commit graph
graph.subtitle: DAG of commits across all branches (local)

collaborators.title: Collaborators
collaborators.subtitle: Co-authored commit pairs (parsed from Co-Authored-By trailers)

specialization.title: Specialization
specialization.subtitle: Languages and directories this author touches most

quality.title: Quality & Security
quality.subtitle: Repo hygiene · secrets · dependencies · code health · authorship
quality.secretsHint: Pattern matches in current HEAD (history scan not run)
quality.groupSecretsHeadHint: Sensitive patterns and risky files in current working tree
quality.groupCodeHint: TODOs, conflict markers, generated artifacts, large blobs
quality.groupAuthorshipHint: Bot share, signed commits, generic emails (last 1000 commits)
quality.conflictMarkersHint: Lines starting with <<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>> in tracked files
quality.generatedFilesHint: Build outputs, lockfiles, minified bundles checked into repo
quality.largeFilesHint: Tracked blobs > 500 KB — consider Git LFS
quality.deepScanHint: Walks every commit and inspects tracked blobs for hard-coded secrets. Slower on large repos.
quality.scoreHint: Composite of repo hygiene, secrets, dependencies, code health, and authorship.
quality.suggestionsHint: Top actionable improvements, ranked by priority.

tip.secretsHigh: {{count}} high-severity secret(s) in HEAD — rotate the keys and remove from the working tree.
tip.secretsMedium: {{count}} possible secret(s) detected — review and remove or move into env files.
tip.riskyFiles: {{count}} risky file(s) committed (.env, key files) — remove and add to .gitignore.
tip.deepScan: Run the deep history scan to check whether secrets ever leaked into git history.
tip.conflicts: {{count}} merge conflict marker(s) committed — resolve them in tracked files.
tip.gitignore: Add a `.gitignore` covering at least .env, node_modules, dist, target.
tip.license: Add a LICENSE file (MIT or Apache-2.0 are common picks).
tip.readme: Add a README — what does this project do and how is it run?
tip.contributing: Add CONTRIBUTING.md so new contributors know how to help.
tip.securityMd: Add SECURITY.md describing how to report vulnerabilities.
tip.editorconfig: Add .editorconfig for consistent editor settings across the team.
tip.lockfile: {{count}} manifest(s) missing a lockfile — pin dependency versions.
tip.largeFiles: {{count}} large file(s) over 500 KB — consider Git LFS or removing them.
tip.generatedFiles: {{count}} generated file(s) committed — add them to .gitignore and rebuild locally.
tip.botHigh: Bot commit share is {{pct}}% — consider filtering bots when measuring human work.
tip.genericEmail: {{count}} author(s) using generic emails — link to real accounts where possible.

commit.title: Commit
commit.selectRepoFirst: Select a repository first to load this commit.
commit.noFileChanges: This commit has no file changes (likely a merge or empty commit).

hotspotsPage.title: Hotspots
hotspotsPage.subtitle: Files, directories and couplings ranked by change frequency
hotspotsPage.couplingHint: Pairs of files that change together (≥ 2 joint commits)
hotspotsPage.riskHint: High churn × concentrated ownership × recent activity

health.title: Repo health
health.subtitle: Composite score from six deterministic sub-scores
health.combinedHint: Combined score from activity health + quality scan (each weighted 50%).
health.subTitle: Activity sub-scores

hint.busFactor: Bus factor = {{value}} — minimum authors covering 50% of code changes
hint.branchesStale: {{stale}} of {{local}} local branches are stale (no commit > 90d, not HEAD)

settings.title: Settings
settings.ideHint: Used by the "Open in editor" buttons throughout the app. The binary must be on your $PATH (e.g. `code`, `cursor`).
settings.terminalHint: Used by the "Open in terminal" button on each repo. macOS choices are launched via `open -a`; on Linux/Windows the binary must be on your $PATH.
settings.gitClientHint: Used by the "Open in git client" button on each repo. macOS apps are launched via `open -a`; Linux/Windows uses the CLI launcher (must be on your $PATH).

netError.descOpenClient:
Network operation failed: {{message}}

Open the repo in your default git client and finish it there?
netError.descNoClient:
Network operation failed: {{message}}

No default git client is set — open Settings to pick one.

launcherIssue.editorNotConfigured: You haven't picked a default editor yet. Open Settings to choose one (VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains, etc.).
launcherIssue.gitClientNotConfigured: You haven't picked a default git client yet. Open Settings to choose one (Tower, Sourcetree, GitKraken, etc.).
launcherIssue.failed: The launch failed: {{message}}. The app may not be installed — check Settings to pick a different one.

repoConfig.title: Configuration
repoConfig.subtitle: Local git configuration. Identity and remotes are editable; everything else is shown read-only.
repoConfig.removeRemoteConfirm: Remove remote "{{name}}"? This only updates the local config.
repoConfig.managedHookHint: Installed by codesight — safe to uninstall from this UI.
repoConfig.uninstallHookConfirm: Remove the codesight-managed "{{name}}" hook? The file will be deleted from .git/hooks.

errors.notARepo: The selected folder is not a git repository.
errors.noReposFound: No git repositories found in the selected folder.

importGraph.title: Import graph
importGraph.subtitle: Static import / use / require dependencies between source files (TS / JS / Rust / Python).
importGraph.hint: Each circle is a source file colored by language; arrows point from importer to imported file. Big nodes are heavily depended on or pull in many modules — natural seams or refactor candidates.
importGraph.empty: No internal imports detected — codesight resolves only repo-relative imports (./foo, @/foo, crate::foo, .foo).
importGraph.summary: {{files}} files scanned · {{edges}} internal edges · {{external}} external imports skipped

ownershipMap.title: Ownership map
ownershipMap.subtitle: Treemap of files sized by total commits, colored by primary author.
ownershipMap.hint: Each rectangle is a file; size shows how often it changes, color shows the author with the most commits on it. Big tiles dominated by one color are concentration risks.
ownershipMap.empty: No file ownership data — make some commits or widen the date range.

couplingsGraph.title: Co-change network
couplingsGraph.subtitle: Files that change together, laid out by force-directed simulation.
couplingsGraph.hint: Each circle is a file; each line is a pair that has changed together at least twice. Heavier lines mean more joint changes; bigger circles mean the file is coupled to more neighbors.
couplingsGraph.empty: No couplings yet — try widening the date range or making more commits.
